evidence_excerpt: Patient 4: By 7 years of age he has poor muscle strength and minimal dystonia and is mostly wheelchair bound ... intermittent dystonic posturing noted at 2 years and 2 months; minimal progression on imaging but persistent functional impairment.

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Ongoing significant motor impairment (mostly wheelchair bound) and dystonia; prior neuropsychological assessment showed severe expressive language impairment and articulation difficulties.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: At ~6 months: on medical review 1 month after a febrile illness at 5 months, he was noted to have truncal hypotonia.
Hospital admission at 9 months with acute gastroenteritis.
Hospital admission at 2 years and 2 months with acute gastroenteritis; he was moderately dehydrated.
During the 2 years and 2 months admission he was noted to have intermittent dystonic posturing.
By 7 years of age he has poor muscle strength and minimal dystonia and is mostly wheelchair bound.
Intense physiotherapy has improved his strength and he recently started cruising.

notes_on_uncertainty: The source summarizes multiple patients with GA I. This extraction focuses on Patient 4, who has the most detailed long-term outcome. Exact diagnosis date is not specified but he was managed per the newborn screening cohort, so diagnosis_date was used as the index. Some outcomes (e.g., developmental delay) may be confounded by a period of neglect; imaging later showed no significant progression, but sustained severe functional limitations persisted.
